frameworks.name: inverse planning theory of mind
frameworks.one_line: rational inference of others' beliefs and desires from observed behavior

frameworks.name: resource-rational heuristics
frameworks.one_line: cheap approximations replace full mentalizing when computation is costly

core_question: When do people engage full belief inference versus heuristic shortcuts in reasoning about others?
key_assumptions: theory of mind is computationally costly
agents can adaptively allocate cognitive resources
belief inference is unnecessary for predicting routine behavior
manipulations: task contexts where belief inference was necessary versus unnecessary for behavior prediction
training on tasks requiring belief sensitivity
key_findings: participants neglected others' beliefs when not needed for behavior prediction
belief neglect was the default mode
belief sensitivity emerged only after training requiring belief inference
interpretations: people adaptively allocate cognitive resources in social reasoning
belief neglect functions as a default heuristic approximating full theory of mind
